design_philosophy.core_loop: Guess drawings before opponent; collect all four colors per level; race to complete all levels
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Speed vs. accuracy—balance rushing to buzz in first against spelling the answer correctly within 25 seconds
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Party game social competition; test knowledge and recognition speed against friends and AI
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid of Pictionary and Trivial Pursuit board games; emphasizes timing-based buzzin mechanics and escalating difficulty through progressive removal of visual/textual aids

difficulty.lives_system: Two-strike lockout per puzzle; incorrect guesses or timeout prevent further guessing until next puzzle
difficulty.checkpointing: Persistent board progression; players retain color credits and position when falling back via Drop-Out Squares on Hard/Very Hard levels
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Four difficulty settings selectable before game start (Easy, Medium, Hard, Very Hard); aids progressively removed—dot patterns, category display, and letter blanks eliminated on harder difficulties

progression.unlock_mechanism: Collect all four category colors per level to advance to next level; three levels on Easy/Medium, four on Hard/Very Hard
replayability_hooks: Four difficulty tiers with different aid removal
Dynamic board layouts with Feature Squares and Drop-Out Squares on higher levels

world_building_techniques: Character tokens (pink high-heeled shoes, bugle, ice cream cone, teddy bear) provide visual identity
Board structure progresses through visual layouts rather than narrative

faction_indicators: Four distinct player tokens with unique visual identities
Four-color category system (blue, green, pink, yellow) creates persistent team/player associations
danger_signals: Feature Squares cause uncontrolled board movement—risk of unfavorable puzzle category placement
Drop-Out Squares on hardest difficulty create setback moments that escalate tension
environmental_storytelling: Board state visually displays player progress and competitive standing
Level-to-level advancement marked by physical board layout changes (Feature Squares appear on level 3+)
Token positions on board serve as persistent competitive narrative
